subject: Anthropology
title: The Fossil Record
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Fossil Record.

hook.text: A skull fragment in African sediment. Three million years old. It once held a brain smaller than yours.

buildup.text: Fossils are mineralized remains of organisms preserved in rock layers, revealing life forms from the distant past.

discovery.text: Dating methods like radiocarbon and potassium-argon reveal when a fossil lived, placing it on the timeline.

twist.text: The fossil record is incomplete. Fossilization is rare, so each discovery is a lucky window into deep time.

climax.text: Every hominin fossil fills a gap in our story. The record is patchy, but the pattern is undeniable.

punchline.text: Bones in stone tell the story of becoming human.

quiz.question: Why is the fossil record incomplete?
options: Fossilization is rare and preservation unlikely
Fossils only form in cold climates
